question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: notify(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the blocking semantics of queue.put() combined with a slow or stalled streamFileParser to cause the downloader thread to block indefinitely on the second call to notify() after the queue reaches capacity, creating a non-network-based DoS that stops all block ingestion?
